vulnerability.reference: Citation-based models amplify existing citation disparities, systematically under-recommending low-cited works.
vulnerability.generated: Researchers and authors rely on recommendation systems that embed unvalidated degree-biased link prediction, leaving them unable to detect or correct for popularity skew.

impact.reference: Entrenches academic hierarchies, stifles novel research, and disadvantages early-career and minority scholars.
impact.generated: Under-cited scholars and papers receive fewer recommendations, deepening citation inequality and narrowing the diversity of scholarly visibility.

dose_response.reference: Overfitting to node degree biases predictions toward high-degree nodes, which in citation-based paper recommendations systematically under-recommends low-cited papers, reinforcing academic inequality.
dose_response.generated: Link prediction models overfit to node degree, through researchers and authors using academic recommendation systems, triggers reliance on unvalidated degree-biased prediction that leaves popularity skew uncorrected, leading to under-cited scholars and papers receiving fewer recommendations, deepening citation inequality and narrowing scholarly visibility diversity.
